name: Climbing
description: A controlled movement state for ascending, descending, or moving across climbable surfaces using surface validation, snap points, stamina, and animation alignment.
design_notes: Climbing depends on trustworthy surfaces and interruption rules. Define climbable_surface_id selection, contact normals, handhold or ledge snap positions, stamina drain, camera framing, detach behavior, and invalid-surface recovery. It should make vertical spaces playable without letting players bypass every authored boundary.
design_purpose: Turn vertical surfaces into navigable routes with readable commitment and recovery.
Create route planning through stamina, handholds, surface tags, or exposed positions.
Support exploration and level design without relying only on stairs, ladders, or jump chains.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable of reading a surface, committing to it, and physically pulling through vertical space.

parameters.name: climb_pace_units_per_second
parameters.description: Travel velocity along the validated climb surface.

parameters.name: climb_stamina_drain_units_per_meter
parameters.description: Stamina consumed while moving along climbable surfaces.

parameters.name: climb_snap_distance_units
parameters.description: Maximum correction distance from character to accepted surface anchor.

parameters.name: climb_surface_angle_min_degrees
parameters.description: Minimum steepness required before the surface is climbable.

parameters.name: ledge_detach_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Buffered jump, drop, or mantle input accepted near ledge transitions.

parameters.name: climb_camera_profile_id
parameters.description: Camera profile used while the player is attached to a climb surface.

edge_cases: A climbable surface can end abruptly, curve inward, or move while the character is attached.
Stamina may deplete while the player is above lethal fall height and needs a readable failure state.
Camera collision can hide the next handhold when the character is close to concave geometry.
Networked climbing needs a shared climbable_surface_id and snap position rather than only client-local ray hits.
common_bugs: Surface validation uses a forward ray only and fails when the character is slightly offset or rotated.
Root motion moves the character away from the stored surface anchor and breaks collision alignment.
Detach leaves gravity disabled or input locked after ledge transition cancellation.
Climb stamina drains during pause, dialogue, or blocked movement because state ownership is split.
balancing_notes: Unlimited free climbing changes world boundary design; tagged surfaces keep routes authored and readable.
Stamina drain should reflect route length and available rest points, not only vertical distance.
Fast climbing supports action pacing, while slower climbing emphasizes exposure and planning.
accessibility_notes: Offer visible climbable-surface highlights or texture language instead of relying on subtle material differences.
Consider climb assists, reduced stamina drain, or forgiving ledge input buffers for long vertical routes.

implementation_notes.unity: Use spherecasts or shape casts to select climbable_surface_id and store the surface normal plus snap position. While climbing, disable ordinary ground locomotion, drive motion along the surface in FixedUpdate, and let IK or animation match the committed body position.
implementation_notes.godot: Query climbable areas with physics shape tests or Area nodes tagged by surface id. Keep traversal_state_id, surface normal, and snap offset on the character, then update climb velocity in _physics_process before applying detach or ledge transitions.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use trace channels, Gameplay Tags, or Smart Objects to validate climbable surfaces. A custom movement mode should own gravity, input projection, stamina cost, and replicated snap transforms while animation montages handle visuals.
implementation_notes.web: Store climb state with surface id, anchor position, normal, and stamina ledger. Resolve movement along the surface in fixed ticks, then handle detach, fall, or mantle as explicit state transitions.

relationships.reason: Climbing often begins from ledge detection, snap points, and blocked-clearance checks.
Climb stamina limits vertical traversal and gives level designers a tunable route constraint.
Climbing requires rewind snapshots for snap points, surface attachment, stamina spend, and animation phase.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses stamina-gated climbing across many outdoor surfaces.

example_games.title: Uncharted 4
example_games.note: Uses authored climbing routes with ledges and handholds.
